steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The noun phrase liquid compounds is followed by an appositive phrase naming hexafluorobenzene and benzene. This appositive identifies which specific compounds have those molar masses—it's essential identifying information.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Appositives that are essential to identifying a noun use no commas.

The compound names identify which compounds, so they must run directly after compounds without punctuation.

Choices A, B, and C all use commas, treating the names as optional information.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
